comment: Round 1: automated Go gate green (gofmt/vet/test -race, incl. every named new test, -count=1). Hands-on live-tmux drive of the Slice B drill card against a scratch fixture found one real render-layer defect (TEST-001, agent-fixable) — FIXED in implement round 3 (viewDrill now renders m.status; new TestDrillStatusIsRendered asserts the hint is in View() output; full suite green). The Slice A hands-on proof (an installed Claude surfacing the CLI) cannot be tester-verified per plan.md and is a user-decision gate (TEST-002, open) — routed to the orchestrator's decision gate, never silently skipped.

issues.title: Drill card status/hint messages (attach 'no session', kill 'no live session', cancelled, kill result, detach) are set on the model but never rendered in the live TUI
issues.description: Verified via a live tmux drive (not just unit tests) against a scratch fixture (scratchpad/drillfix, a copy of cli/internal/contract/testdata/repo plus a hand-built .gogo/resources/cli/sessions/*.json registry and decoy tmux sessions). Drilled into 'legacy-ready' (a card with only a stale/reaped tracked session, no live tmux) and pressed 'a': the pane was byte-identical before/after — no hint appeared — even though attachFeature (cli/internal/tui/update.go ~line 433) sets `m.status = "no running session for " + f.Slug`. Pressed 'K' on the same card: same silent no-op — killDrill (update.go ~line 462) sets `m.status = "no live session to kill for " + f.Slug`, but no confirm opens and no hint is shown. Root cause confirmed by drilling into 'inprogress' (a card with a live tracked session), opening the K confirm, cancelling with Esc: correctly returned to the drill card (REV-001's fix verified live), but only after backing all the way out to the board did the status line there read 'cancelled' — proving m.status WAS set correctly by finishKill (update.go ~line 495) but was never rendered while mode==modeDrill; it only became visible once back on the board, because viewBoard() (view.go ~lines 44-49) explicitly renders `status := m.status; if status == "" { status = m.boardStatusLine() }` above its help line, while viewDrill() (view.go ~lines 270-330) has no equivalent status line at all. The same gap silences the post-kill confirmation (`m.status = "killed %d session(s)"`, update.go ~lines 514-517) and the post-detach confirmation (`"detached from " + session`) whenever they land while still in the drill. Net effect: FR-B3's explicit spec item 3 ('no live session → a status hint, no attach') is satisfied at the MODEL layer only — TestDrillAttachWiring/TestDrillKillWiring correctly assert `Model.status` and pass — but the live TUI shows the user literally nothing for any of these five distinct status messages, which reads as a frozen/unresponsive key rather than a deliberate, informative no-op. This is exactly the class of bug test-strategy.md's 'Go TUI — unit tests are NOT enough' override calls out: a model-level assertion passed while View() was never exercised for this path.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. Give viewDrill() the same status-line handling viewBoard() already has: render `statusStyle(m.status)` (or an equivalent) just above the help line in viewDrill() (cli/internal/tui/view.go, right before the `help := ...` at ~line 328), mirroring viewBoard()'s `status := m.status; if status == "" { status = m.boardStatusLine() }` pattern (a drill-appropriate fallback, or empty, is fine when m.status == ""). Then extend TestDrillCardDetailRender (or add a focused test) to assert View() contains the hint text after driving 'a'/'K' with no live session, and after a completed kill ('killed 1 session') and a cancelled one ('cancelled') — closing the render-coverage gap the existing Model-only assertions left open.
issues.fix_summary: viewDrill() (cli/internal/tui/view.go) now appends statusStyle(m.status) above the help line when m.status != "", mirroring viewBoard() — so the a/K 'no session' hints, the kill confirmations, and detach messages are visible while mode==modeDrill. New TestDrillStatusIsRendered drives `a` with no live session and asserts the hint appears in View() output (not just Model.status). gofmt/vet/go test -race ./... green.

issues.title: Hands-on proof of Slice A CLI-awareness (an installed Claude surfaces the CLI when asked to manage/view work) — user-decision gate, cannot be tester-verified
issues.description: plan.md's Acceptance signal for Part A states explicitly: 'The hands-on proof (an installed Claude surfacing the CLI) is a user-decision check, never a silent skip.' Verifying whether a SEPARATE installed Claude instance, in a gogo project with the `gogo` binary on PATH, actually loads/consults skills/gogo-cli/SKILL.md and suggests the CLI in response to a natural-language ask (e.g. 'manage my gogo work') is a live skill-selection behaviour of a different Claude session — not a property of any file or artifact this tester can assert deterministically, and this tester's own invocation (a narrow phase-④ testing task, not a general 'manage my gogo work' session) does not exercise the same skill-loading heuristics a real ask would. Every artifact-level prerequisite for this behaviour IS verified and green: skills/gogo-cli/SKILL.md exists with a frontmatter description written to trigger on CLI relevance (confirmed by direct read); skills/gogo/SKILL.md carries the lean 'Load when: the gogo CLI is relevant ... → skills/gogo-cli' pointer (confirmed by direct read); the enumeration-sync lint (TestCLICommandEnumerationInSync) is green and non-tautological (parses main.go's real dispatch switch, checks 4 independent sources including printHelp itself per the REV-002 fix). What remains unverifiable by this tester is the live behavioural proof.
issues.proposed_solution: NEEDS-USER-DECISION. In a gogo project with the gogo binary on PATH (e.g. `cd cli && go build -o gogo .` in this repo, or the curl install), start a FRESH Claude Code session (not this one) and ask something like 'manage my gogo work' / 'what's the status of my gogo features' / 'help me view my gogo board', then confirm Claude (a) surfaces/uses the gogo-cli skill (visible in its response, e.g. it mentions or suggests running `gogo` / `gogo status` / `gogo view`) and (b) actually suggests the CLI rather than defaulting only to the in-chat /gogo:* flow. Options for the user: (1) run this check now and report pass/fail so this issue can be marked verified or reopened with a wording fix; (2) explicitly skip this check for now — per the gogo-test skill, 'a hands-on check is only ever skipped when the user says so'; (3) if it fails, the fix is an implement-round wording/description tweak to skills/gogo-cli/SKILL.md's frontmatter trigger and/or the skills/gogo/SKILL.md pointer, not a code change.
issues.fix_summary: User-skipped per decision D6 (2026-07-12): the artifact-level prerequisites (gogo-cli skill + trigger-worded frontmatter, the lean Load-when pointer, the green non-tautological enumeration-sync lint) are all verified; the remaining live behavioural proof (an installed Claude surfacing the CLI) will be confirmed opportunistically in real CLI use. Only the user may skip a hands-on check — recorded, not silently dropped.
